title: HLA coding Ti/Tv = 0.67–1.54 (vs genome coding baseline ~3.0); HLA non-coding Ti/Tv = 0.51–0.73 (vs neutral baseline ~2.0) — the transversion-enriched signature of diversifying selection

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'HLA coding Ti/Tv = 0.67–1.54 (vs genome coding baseline ~3.0); HLA non-coding Ti'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: All 4-field alleles at 6 HLA loci; per-bp substitutions against the *01:01:01:01 reference are classified as transitions (purine→purine, pyrimidine→pyrimidine) or transversions (across purine/pyrimidine).

premises.note: 22253 = sum of n_alleles across 6 loci from titv_per_compartment.csv (coding rows).

source.note: Genome-wide Ti/Tv baselines: coding ~3.0 (purifying selection signature), non-coding ~2.0 (neutral mutation spectrum). Curated from 1000 Genomes Phase 3 published summaries.

operations.note: Compute (transitions / transversions) per locus × compartment. Values in titv_per_compartment.csv.

statistics.name: per_locus_coding_titv
statistics.note: Per-locus coding Ti/Tv. Range 0.67–1.54. HLA-B at 0.67 is the minimum — most transversion-enriched and therefore most strongly amino-acid-changing.

statistics.name: per_locus_noncoding_titv
statistics.note: Per-locus non-coding Ti/Tv. Range 0.51–0.73. DRB1 at 0.51 is the extremum.

statistics.name: hla_coding_titv_max
statistics.note: DPB1 coding Ti/Tv is the HLA-wide maximum — still far below the genome coding baseline.

statistics.name: hla_noncoding_titv_max
statistics.note: HLA-B non-coding Ti/Tv is the HLA-wide non-coding maximum.

statistics.name: genome_wide_coding_titv_baseline
statistics.note: Curated genome coding Ti/Tv baseline (purifying selection). The signal is the excess transversions over this baseline.

statistics.name: genome_wide_noncoding_titv_baseline
statistics.note: Curated genome non-coding Ti/Tv baseline (neutral mutation spectrum).

inference.justification: Three conjuncts license the diversifying-selection signature: (1) HLA coding Ti/Tv maximum (1.54 at DPB1) < genome coding baseline (~3.0) — stat-vs-stat — establishes that every HLA locus is transversion-enriched relative to typical coding sequences, the expected signature of selection favoring amino-acid-changing mutations; (2) HLA non-coding Ti/Tv maximum (0.73) < genome non-coding baseline (~2.0) — stat-vs-stat — confirms the transversion enrichment extends into non-coding regions, consistent with linked selection / tight coupling to the coding-selected peptide-binding groove; (3) HLA coding Ti/Tv < 2.0 (absolute threshold) adds a redundant anchor that the pattern holds even if the curated baseline drifted.
inference.failure_mode: If the HLA coding maximum rose above the genome baseline, the 'transversion enrichment' claim would collapse to 'typical selection'. If the HLA non-coding maximum rose above the non-coding baseline, the linked-selection interpretation would weaken because non-coding would look locally neutral.

conclusion.assertion: Across all 6 HLA loci, coding Ti/Tv ratios (0.67–1.54) fall well below the genome-wide coding baseline (~3.0) — a transversion-enriched signature of diversifying / positive selection on peptide-binding residues. HLA non-coding Ti/Tv ratios (0.51–0.73) are also below the neutral baseline (~2.0), indicating that the transversion enrichment extends beyond coding into linked non-coding sequence. HLA-B coding Ti/Tv = 0.67 is the minimum — the locus most strongly shaped by amino-acid-changing selection.

scope.scale_note: Per-locus × per-compartment aggregate. Per-feature heatmap (E1/I1/E2/…/3'UTR) is in titv_per_feature.csv and is a child fixture, not pinned here.

confidence.note: No p-value — the claim is a comparison of estimated Ti/Tv ratios against published genome baselines. Saturation correction is open in NEG_HARDENING_PLAN #16.

external_assumptions.statement: Ti/Tv ratios computed against the *01:01:01:01 reference are representative of the true allelic diversity; alternative reference choices would shift values modestly but preserve the relative ordering.

Genome-wide coding Ti/Tv ~3.0 and non-coding ~2.0 are faithful baselines (Anderson & Karolchik; 1000G Phase 3). Baseline uncertainty is on the order of ±0.2, which does not change the qualitative conclusion.

Alternative explanations for low Ti/Tv — e.g., gene conversion, local mutation-rate heterogeneity, repeat-driven saturation — cannot fully account for the observed depth of the transversion enrichment across all 6 loci and both compartments. The signature is broadly consistent with balancing / diversifying selection but the outcome is qualified because saturation correction is OPEN.
